name: Goerke Park
description: Come play pickleball at Goerke Park in Stevens Point, WI! There are 3 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. Players have access to locker rooms, restrooms, and water.

name: Mead Park
description: Mead Park is a place to play pickleball in Stevens Point, WI. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are free. You can’t reserve the courts.

name: Plover Boys and Girls Club: Easlan-Weslan Park
description: Plover Boys and Girls Club: Easlan-Weslan Park is a place to play pickleball in Plover, WI. There are 2 indoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. A membership is required to play. Players have access to restrooms.

name: Sentry Pickleball Complex at Lake Pacawa Park
description: Sentry Pickleball Complex at Lake Pacawa Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Plover, WI. There are 12 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, lights, trainers or lessons, wheelchair accessibility, and adaptive programming.
open_play_schedule: Open Play 7-12/Free Play 12-4:30/Open/Free Play 4:30-10  During Free Play time, groups of 4 may stay together.  Open Play times are 4 on/4 off

name: SentryWorld Feildhouse
description: SentryWorld Feildhouse is a place to play pickleball in Stevens Point, WI. There are 15 indoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to food, locker rooms, restrooms, water, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, trainers or lessons, and wheelchair accessibility. The courts are free. Reservations are available.

name: Stevens Point YMCA
description: Stevens Point YMCA is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Stevens Point, WI. There are 4 indoor wood courts. Tape lines have been laid, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Players have access to locker rooms, restrooms, water, and youth programming.
